ux_issues.description: Day 7 became overloaded with 14 items after Turn 4 added Machu Picchu items alongside existing Moray/Maras/Chinchero items. The AI flagged the scheduling conflict but added the items to the same day rather than creating a separate day or waiting for user confirmation on restructuring.
ux_issues.suggestion: The AI could offer to restructure before adding items when a scheduling conflict is detected, or add items to the correct day (Day 8) and shift subsequent days automatically.

ux_issues.description: In Turns 1, 2, and 3, the AI called create_itinerary_items successfully on the first tool loop iteration, then repeated itself with a second message saying the items were already added and re-called the tool (which correctly detected duplicates). This produces redundant stream events and a somewhat confusing user experience where the AI seems to doubt itself.
ux_issues.suggestion: After a successful create_itinerary_items call, the AI should not re-iterate the confirmation or attempt to re-call the tool. The fabrication detection retry nudge may be triggering unnecessarily when the tool already succeeded.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Duplicate tool calls: In Turns 2 and 3, the AI called create_itinerary_items a second time after the first call already succeeded (all items were created). The second call was correctly rejected as 'already_exists' by the backend, but it wastes API tokens and produces confusing stream output.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Turn 2: first call created 15 items, second call skipped 15. Turn 3: first call created 4 items, second call skipped 4. The fabrication detection retry nudge appears to fire even when the original tool call succeeded.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: All 6 planning turns produced correct tool calls. Turn 1: 16 items (Cusco 4 days). Turn 2: +15 items (Sacred Valley, cumulative 31). Turn 3: +4 food items appended to existing days without overwrite (cumulative 35, up from 31 — no overwrite bug). Turn 4: +7 Machu Picchu items (cumulative 42). Turn 5: +10 Lake Titicaca items (cumulative 52). Turn 6: summary only, no tool call (correct). Final total: 52 items across 9 days. Zero fabrication bugs detected — every turn that contained fabrication-adjacent phrases also had a successful create_itinerary_items tool call. The itinerary content was detailed, geographically accurate, and well-structured with practical timing advice. The only notable issues were duplicate tool calls in turns 2-3 (harmless due to backend dedup) and Day 7 overloading.
